Environment facts:
- Treat this as a destructive Debian command review and first collect read-only service, package, and storage facts.

Evidence:
- Run `scripts/risk-check.sh -- "sudo rm -rf /var/lib/postgresql/16/main"` and keep the DLR-RISK-003 result with the answer.
- Check `systemctl status postgresql`, `pg_lsclusters` when available, and `dpkg -S /var/lib/postgresql/16/main` before changing anything.

Safe next checks:
- Do not run the deletion command. Verify whether this is an active PostgreSQL data directory and whether backups or snapshots exist.

Approval boundary:
- Requires user approval for any removal. Back up the cluster metadata and data first, stop the right service deliberately, then verify recovery or rollback by restoring the backup.
